# Implementation Plan: Docling Conversion Service

**Branch**: `001-docling-conversion-service` | **Date**: 2025-12-23 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)\
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/001-docling-conversion-service/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by the `/speckit.plan` command. See `.specify/templates/commands/plan.md` for the execution workflow.

## Summary

Convert KaraKeep bookmarks (HTML/PDF/arXiv/GitHub) to Markdown using Docling with durable state tracking, idempotency, and S3 artifact storage. KaraKeep bookmark is the source of truth; validate bookmarks have required content (HTML/text/PDF). For arXiv sources, handle abstract page links (resolve `arxiv_id` and download via `AsyncArxivClient.download_paper_pdf(arxiv_id)`), PDF assets (use provided or fetch from KaraKeep), and link bookmarks with HTML content (resolve `arxiv_id` and download via client; use `arxiv_pdf_url` when present). Implement FastAPI REST API with SQLite job queue, 4 parallel Docling workers, exponential backoff retry logic, and HTML-only Web UI for operational monitoring. Support reprocessing with payload versioning and content-based deduplication via markdown hashing.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Python 3.12+ (managed via uv)\
**Primary Dependencies**: FastAPI, Docling (v2.65+), SQLModel, boto3 (S3 client), xxhash, httpx, pydantic-settings, karakeep_client (from .venv)\
**Storage**: SQLite with WAL mode via SQLModel; S3-compatible object storage for artifacts\
**Testing**: pytest with fixtures for deterministic transforms, contract tests for API, integration tests for S3 uploads\
**Target Platform**: Linux server (Docker/compose), macOS development (via Nix/uv devshell)\
**Project Type**: Single backend service (FastAPI REST API + background workers + HTML UI)\
**Performance Goals** (arbitrary, internal expectations):

- Single HTML bookmark (\<5MB): conversion complete within 120 seconds
- PDF bookmark (20 pages): conversion complete within 5 minutes
- 4 concurrent conversions without degradation
- API endpoints: \<100ms response for job submission/query
- Idempotency check: \<100ms for duplicate detection

**Constraints**:

- Fetch timeout: 30s (HTML), with retry backoff (3 attempts)
- Size caps: 100MB for HTML, 200MB for PDF
- Bounded concurrency: 4 parallel Docling workers (configurable)
- SQLite single-writer pattern with lock retry logic
- Memory: ~8GB RAM minimum for 4 concurrent Docling processes
- Temp workspace: 10GB minimum disk space

**Scale/Scope**:

- Internal-only deployment (no internet-facing auth required)
- Expected throughput: hundreds of bookmarks per day
- 4 parallel conversion workers
- Job history retention: retain all aside from user-directed archive/purge strategy for old jobs via the HTML webui
- S3 bucket quota: assume unlimited for initial deployment
- **Bookmark Metadata**: KaraKeep bookmark is source of truth; must contain HTML content, text, or PDF asset. Bookmarks track `content_type` (html/pdfвЂ”format detected from KaraKeep structure) and `source_type` (arxiv/github/otherвЂ”origin parsed from URL). arXiv bookmarks: abstract page links resolve `arxiv_id` and download via `AsyncArxivClient.download_paper_pdf(arxiv_id)`; PDF assets use provided bytes or fetch from KaraKeep; link bookmarks with HTML resolve `arxiv_id` (use `arxiv_pdf_url` when present) and download via client. GitHub bookmarks are always HTML. All other links are expected to be HTML.

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

- **G1 Data provenance**:

  - Sources: KaraKeep bookmarks (via karakeep_id, url, content/text/assetsвЂ”validated for presence), arXiv (via arxiv_id in URL or arxiv_pdf_url in metadata), GitHub (via owner/repo in URL)
  - Metadata: bookmarks table stores karakeep_id, url, normalized_url, source_type, title, aizk_uuid
  - Hashing: xxhash64 of normalized Markdown content (markdown_hash_xx64) for deduplication and change detection
  - Raw inputs: source HTML/PDF retained only temporarily in fetch stage; conversion_outputs.manifest_key references all generated artifacts
  - Replay: conversion_outputs records docling_version, pipeline_name, payload_version, config_hash enabling re-conversion with same parameters
  - **Status**: вњ… PASS

- **G2 Reproducibility**:

  - Pinned dependencies: Docling v2.65+ pinned in pyproject.toml; uv lock ensures deterministic resolution
  - Environment: Runs in Nix/uv devshell; dependencies declared in pyproject.toml
  - Idempotency: idempotency_key computed from hash(aizk_uuid + payload_version + docling_version + config_hash); duplicate submissions return existing job without re-processing
  - Seeds: Docling pipelines are deterministic given same inputs; no random seeds required
  - Side effects: Job submission checks idempotency_key before creating new record; S3 uploads happen only after job completion with atomic status update in transaction
  - **Status**: вњ… PASS

- **G3 Test-first**:

  - Contract tests: API endpoint schemas (POST /v1/jobs, GET /v1/jobs/{id}, GET /v1/outputs/{uuid}, POST /v1/jobs/batch, POST /v1/jobs/actions) tested against OpenAPI spec
  - Fixture tests: Deterministic Markdown normalization, URL normalization, idempotency_key computation, markdown_hash_xx64 computation with known inputs/outputs
  - Integration tests: S3 upload verification with local S3-compatible test instance, SQLite transaction isolation, job status transitions, retry logic
  - Regression tests: Edge cases from spec (404s, empty Markdown, duplicate idempotency_key, S3 failures) covered before merge
  - **Status**: вњ… PASS (tests to be added during implementation phase per TDD)

- **G4 Privacy & safety**:

  - No PII: Bookmarks contain only URLs, titles, identifiers (no user authentication or personal data); content is from public internet so PII is not expected
  - Redaction: Not applicable (public web content only)
  - Access controls: Internal-only deployment behind localhost/VPN; no internet-facing endpoints
  - External APIs: Fetches content from any URL specified in bookmarks (unrestricted); primary sources include arXiv, GitHub, and general web content
  - Secrets: S3 credentials, API keys loaded from environment variables via pydantic-settings; no secrets in repo or logs
  - **Status**: вњ… PASS

- **G5 Observability & versioning**:

  - Structured logging: All processing events include aizk_uuid, job_id, karakeep_id, status as context fields using Python logging with structured formatters
  - Metrics: Emit queue depth, job duration histogram, job status counts (SUCCEEDED/FAILED/CANCELLED), fetch latency, S3 upload latency
  - Versioning: conversion_outputs records payload_version, docling_version; manifest.json includes artifact list and metadata
  - Migration: Payload version bumps require migration note in CHANGELOG.md; markdown_hash_xx64 enables detection of meaningful content changes
  - **Status**: вњ… PASS

- **G6 ADRs**:

  - ADR-001: S3 storage strategy and atomic finalization using database transactions (documented in research.md)
  - ADR-002: Idempotency and payload_version semantics for reprocessing (documented in research.md)
  - ADR-003: SQLite with WAL mode for initial deployment (documented in research.md)
  - Note: These decisions have been elevated to spec-level implementation directives; see spec.md Technical Context & Implementation Directives section
  - **Status**: вњ… PASS (ADRs documented in research.md)

- **G7 Governance**:

  - Changelog: CHANGELOG.md will be updated following keepachangelog.com format (Added/Changed/Fixed sections)
  - Semantic Versioning: Feature release will bump MINOR version (0.0.1 в†’ 0.1.0)
  - Conventional Commits: PR commits/title will use `feat(conversion):` prefix
  - **Status**: вњ… PASS

- **G8 Preconditions**:

  - вњ… spec.md approved with all requirements defined
  - вњ… plan.md complete with implementation roadmap
  - вњ… All constitution gates passed
  - вњ… Ready for Phase 0 research and Phase 1 design


**Structure Decision**: Single backend project structure selected. This is a standalone service (not web app or mobile) providing REST API and background workers. Conversion service is self-contained within `src/aizk/conversion/` package to maintain clean separation from existing AIZK codebase components (ai/, datamodel/, utilities/). Tests mirror source structure under `tests/conversion/`.
